title: All Hail
subtitle: The risen Lord meets the three Marys on the road, and they hold his feet

stillness: As Anselm says: as long as you are able to pray,
let no sleep break in upon your delight;
let no outward tumult hinder you.
Be still, and seek him whom your soul loves.
gospel: And behold, Jesus met them, saying:
Then Jesus said to them: Fear not.
Go, tell my brethren that they go into Galilee;
behold: While the three Marys went on together,
before they reached the city and the Apostles,
behold, the Lord Jesus met them and appeared to them.
With a tender greeting the Lord raised up the downcast,
and with a word of consolation he consoled the sorrowing,
saying: All hail — forming Ave out of Eva.
And they, glad beyond what can be told, came near,
now believing and enlightened through faith,
and they held, and kissed his feet out of devotion,
proving openly the humanity in which he had risen,
and made certain of his Resurrection;
and they adored God and the Lord with the adoration of latria.
consider: As Chrysostom says: they held his feet,
and running to him with overflowing joy,
they received, even by touch, the certitude of the Resurrection,
and they adored him.
Perhaps one of you would wish to be made like those women,
and to hold the feet of Jesus.
You can even now, whoever you will —
not the feet and hands only, but even the head —
hold that sacred One,
partaking of the awe-filled mysteries with a pure conscience.
This is the way of coming to pardon:
by drawing near to the feet of Christ.
We must go to kiss the foot of Christ by devout prayer,
considering, that is, his humanity.
If you would grasp the loftiness of God,
grasp first the humanity of Christ.
And see here: the Master of humility calls his disciples brethren,
naming them more tenderly than was his custom,
that they might draw near to him more securely, and not fear —
for they had abandoned him at his seizing.
Never did Jesus, the Lord of all, lay aside humility;
did he not at the last wash the disciples' feet?
Was he not humbled beyond what can be told,
bearing the gibbet of the cross?
Without this foundation, the building of the other virtues is raised in vain.
Trust not in chastity, nor in poverty, nor in any virtue, without humility.
She who once gave death to man in paradise —
from the tomb the woman announced life to men;
she who had been the gate of death is the first to preach the resurrection,
and shows forth the gate of life.
Most merciful is the tenderness of God here shown toward womankind:
that the ancient error of the transgression might be wiped away,
and the guilt of the human race be cut off whence it came.
prayer: O Jesus, our redemption, our love and our longing,
who, rising from the dead, willed in your mercy to meet and appear
to the devout women seeking you, desiring you, returning from your tomb:
I seek you, whom my soul loves, O sweetest Jesus;
I seek, and I do not find.
Come then to my soul that seeks you, O only Son of God the Father,
and if you withhold your desirable presence now,
do not take it away in the time to come;
that in the last resurrection of the faithful
you may show yourself to me, with them, gracious and kind,
that I may be worthy to rejoice in your presence forever

latinJewel.latin: Mulier, quæ fuerat janua mortis, prima prædicat resurrectionem, et ostendit januam vitæ.
latinJewel.english: The woman, who had been the gate of death, is the first to preach the resurrection, and shows forth the gate of life.
